interactions.summary: Jiang says the Red Heifer sequence points to a roughly one- or two-year window for Third Temple consecration.
The host adds right-wing media chatter about the Red Heifer and suggests persecution dynamics and shocking acts are part of the plan.
Jiang disputes the timing claim, insists the mosque must fall first, and then begins a broader framework for Jewish history and identity under pressure.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts a one- or two-year window for the Red Heifer sacrifice and Third Temple consecration sequence.
Jiang is skeptical the Red Heifer has already been sacrificed because he thinks the site must first be cleared by destruction of the mosque and only then ritually consecrated.
Jiang says Kabbalah and numerology make the destruction of the mosque part of a long-standing timed plan rather than an improvisation.
Jiang introduces a broader framework in which Jewish history is structured by managing identity, hatred, and cohesion under persecution.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang insists the ritual order matters: blow up the mosque first, consecrate later.
signature_moments.source_phrase: You first need to destroy that mosque.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sharpens his view that prophecy is followed as a script rather than merely referenced symbolically.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The ritual sign Jiang links to consecrating a future Third Temple after the existing mosque is removed.
The temple Jiang says end-times actors are trying to consecrate through a staged ritual sequence.

uncertainty_notes.note: The host cites a media figure and a likely ASR-damaged proper name when describing the claimed sacrifice announcement; the semantic extraction keeps only the substance of the claim.
